source_book: DC Pandey Class 12 Ch.4 Moving Charges and Magnetism §4.10 (Moving coil galvanometer) — table-of-contents reference only; teaching authored from first principles. NCERT consulted for the Indian real-world anchor only. The atomic concept taught here is the MECHANISM of the moving-coil galvanometer: an N-turn coil between magnet poles carries current I, so the BIL force on its two vertical sides forms a COUPLE — equal-opposite forces on different lines give ΣF = 0 (the coil turns, never slides) and a deflecting torque τ = NIAB. A RADIAL field (concave pole faces + a soft-iron core) keeps the coil plane always parallel to B, so its sides stay perpendicular to B at EVERY deflection and τ stays NIAB with no sinθ fade — without it τ = NIAB·sinθ and the scale would crowd. A hairspring supplies a restoring torque τ = kφ; at equilibrium NIAB = kφ, so φ = (NAB/k)·I is LINEAR in current (a uniform scale), and the current sensitivity φ/I = NAB/k is a fixed device constant. The deepest student confusions targeted: (M1) the coil slides across the gap; (M2) any field gives a proportional reading; (M3) the pole-gap field is straight/uniform; (M4) nothing stops the coil; (M5) the coil swings all the way to 90°; (M6) the radial scale is crowded too; (M7) sensitivity depends on the current measured. CUT-LINE: this concept teaches the mechanism ONLY — it does NOT cover converting the galvanometer into an ammeter (shunt) or voltmeter (series resistance), nor voltage sensitivity φ/V = NAB/kR; those are the downstream concept galvanometer_to_ammeter_voltmeter. It re-uses, not re-derives, the couple τ = NIAB (prerequisite torque_on_current_loop_in_field) and the single-side force F = BIL (prerequisite force_on_current_carrying_wire). Conceptual-only ship (no mode_overrides). EPIC-L-first (no epic_c_branches; the seven misconceptions are confronted inside EPIC-L via Rule 16a misconception_watch + predict→reveal).

real_world_anchor.primary: Picture the analog galvanometer sitting on the bench in your school or college physics lab — the brass-cased instrument with a thin pointer that flicks across a paper scale the moment current flows. Inside it is exactly this: an N-turn coil wound on a soft-iron core, hung by a hairspring between two curved magnet poles. You have read its scale in your practical exam without ever knowing why those scale divisions are perfectly evenly spaced — that gap is the whole question this concept answers.
real_world_anchor.secondary: Picture an older analog needle meter — the kind on a non-digital multimeter an electrician still carries, or the swinging needle on a battery tester. Same moving coil, same fine pointer, same evenly-spaced scale: more current swings the needle further, and equal jumps in current move it in equal steps.
real_world_anchor.tertiary: Picture the swinging VU meter on an old audio amplifier or tape deck, its needle bouncing with the music. That bouncing needle is one more moving coil in a radial field, turning against a hairspring — read it and you are reading τ = NIAB balanced by kφ.

N.name: Number of turns in the coil

B.name: Radial magnetic field in the gap

A.name: Area of the coil face (A = L*b)

formulas.force_per_side: F = N B I L  — BIL force on each vertical side, N turns.
formulas.net_force: Sum(F) = 0  — equal-opposite forces on different lines form a couple (no slide).
formulas.tau_deflect_radial: tau = N I A B  — radial field: coil plane always parallel to B, so sin(theta) == 1 at every deflection (NO angle factor).
formulas.tau_uniform_field: tau = N I A B sin(theta)  — uniform field only; theta = angle(mu, B) = 90 deg - phi, so tau = N I A B cos(phi) and the scale is crowded.
formulas.tau_spring: tau = k phi  — hairspring restoring torque, proportional to deflection phi.
formulas.equilibrium: N I A B = k phi  ->  phi = (N A B / k) * I  — deflection is exactly linear in current.
formulas.current_sensitivity: S_I = phi / I = N A B / k  — a fixed device constant (rad/A); raise N, A, B or lower k.

constraints: Sum(F) = 0 on the coil at all times: the two side-forces are equal, opposite, and on different lines - a pure couple, so the coil turns, never slides.
On the RADIAL path tau = N I A B carries NO sin(theta) factor: the coil plane is always parallel to B, so the sides stay perpendicular to B (theta = 90 deg) at every deflection.
In a UNIFORM field tau = N I A B sin(theta) with theta = 90 deg - phi, so tau falls as cos(phi) and equal current steps give unequal swings (non-uniform scale).
At equilibrium N I A B = k phi, so phi = (N A B / k) * I - deflection is exactly proportional to current (uniform scale).
Current sensitivity phi/I = N A B / k depends only on the device (N, A, B, k) - never on the current being measured.
B is the radial gap field (revealed S4+); area A = L*b is exposed as one knob; current I is the measured input in the microamp range.

aha_moment.statement: Radial field keeps the coil square to B, so torque stays NIAB at every angle.
aha_moment.visual_confirmation: STATE_4 morphs the straight field to radial and curves the poles; as the coil turns through any angle the two force arrows hold full length and the deflecting-torque arrow stays the same length — no sinθ fade.

STATE_1.title: A coil between magnet poles — how does it read current?

scene_composition.text: A galvanometer: an N-turn coil on a soft-iron core, between two magnet poles

Amber dots = current I
up one side, down the other
N turns on the soft-iron core

Like the analog meter
in your physics lab —
the pointer rests at zero.
Question: how does this turn current into a steady reading on the scale?

tts_sentences.text_en: Here is a galvanometer. An N-turn coil hangs on a soft-iron core between two magnet poles.
tts_sentences.text_hi: यहाँ एक galvanometer है। एक N-turn coil, दो magnet poles के बीच एक soft-iron core पर लटकी हुई है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇక్కడ ఒక galvanometer ఉంది. ఒక N-turn coil, రెండు magnet poles మధ్యలో ఉన్న ఒక soft-iron core పై వేలాడుతోంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Current I flows through the coil — watch it march up one side and down the other.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Coil में Current I बहता है — देखिए यह एक तरफ ऊपर और दूसरी तरफ नीचे जाता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: Coil ద్వారా Current I ప్రవహిస్తుంది — ఇది ఒక వైపు పైకి, మరొక వైపు కిందికి వెళ్లడాన్ని గమనించండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: The pointer rests at zero. How does this turn into a current meter?
tts_sentences.text_hi: Pointer zero पर टिका है। यह एक current meter में कैसे बदलता है?
tts_sentences.text_te: Pointer zero వద్ద విశ్రాంతిగా ఉంది. ఇది ఒక current meter గా ఎలా మారుతుంది?

STATE_2.title: The couple: F = NBIL on each side, τ = NIAB, ΣF = 0

scene_composition.text: Each vertical side carries current I across the field B

Predict before the reveal:
which way is each side pushed —
and does the coil slide across
the gap, or turn in place?

Left side: into the page.
Right side: out of the page.
Equal, opposite, different lines
→ ΣF = 0 (no slide), pure twist.
F = NBIL per side    →    deflecting torque τ = NIAB    (ΣF = 0)

tts_sentences.text_en: The field B crosses the gap. Each vertical side of the coil carries current I straight through that field.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Field B gap को पार करता है। Coil का प्रत्येक vertical side उस field से सीधे current I ले जाता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: Field B, gap ను దాటుతుంది. Coil యొక్క ప్రతి vertical side ఆ field గుండా current I ని నేరుగా తీసుకువెళుతుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Which way is each side pushed — and does the coil slide across the gap, or turn in place?
tts_sentences.text_hi: प्रत्येक side को किस दिशा में धकेला जाता है — और क्या coil gap के आर-पार slide करती है, या अपनी जगह पर घूमती है?
tts_sentences.text_te: ప్రతి side ఏ వైపుకు నెట్టబడుతుంది — మరియు coil gap అంతటా slide అవుతుందా, లేక అక్కడే తిరుగుతుందా?

tts_sentences.text_en: Right-hand rule: the left side is forced into the page, the right side out of the page.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Right-hand rule: left side को page के अंदर धकेला जाता है, right side को page के बाहर।
tts_sentences.text_te: Right-hand rule: left side page లోపలికి నెట్టబడుతుంది, right side page బయటకు.

tts_sentences.text_en: Equal and opposite forces on different lines cannot slide the coil — net force is zero — but they twist it.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अलग-अलग lines पर equal और opposite forces coil को slide नहीं कर सकते — net force zero है — लेकिन वे इसे twist करते हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: వేర్వేరు lines పై equal మరియు opposite forces coil ను slide చేయలేవు — net force zero — కానీ అవి దానిని twist చేస్తాయి.

tts_sentences.text_en: The force per side is F equals N B I L, and together they give the deflecting torque tau equals N I A B.
tts_sentences.text_hi: प्रति side force है F equals N B I L, और साथ मिलकर वे deflecting torque tau equals N I A B देते हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: ప్రతి side కు force F equals N B I L, మరియు అవి కలిసి deflecting torque tau equals N I A B ను ఇస్తాయి.

misconception_watch.belief: The coil slides or translates across the gap when current flows.
misconception_watch.visual_counter: The left-side force draws into the page and the right-side force out of the page, then a ΣF = 0 badge appears while the coil turns in place.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: Equal and opposite forces on different lines form a couple: ΣF = 0 so the coil cannot slide — it only twists, with torque τ = NIAB.

STATE_3.title: The sinθ trap: a uniform field gives a crowded scale

scene_composition.text: Suppose the field were uniform — straight, between two flat poles

Step the current up in
equal jumps. Will the pointer
move in equal steps —
or not?

As the coil turns, its sides
tilt away from square-to-B,
so each force shrinks and the
swing gets smaller every step.
Uniform field: τ = NIAB·sinθ → equal current steps, unequal swings → a crowded scale

tts_sentences.text_en: Suppose for now the field were uniform — straight lines between two flat poles.
tts_sentences.text_hi: मान लीजिए अभी field uniform था — दो flat poles के बीच straight lines।
tts_sentences.text_te: ప్రస్తుతానికి field uniform గా ఉందని అనుకుందాం — రెండు flat poles మధ్య straight lines.

tts_sentences.text_en: Step the current up in equal jumps. Will the pointer move in equal steps, or not?
tts_sentences.text_hi: Current को equal jumps में ऊपर बढ़ाएँ। क्या pointer equal steps में चलेगा, या नहीं?
tts_sentences.text_te: Current ను equal jumps లో పెంచండి. Pointer equal steps లో కదులుతుందా, లేదా?

tts_sentences.text_en: As the coil turns, its sides tilt away from square-to-B, so each force shrinks and the torque falls with sine theta.
tts_sentences.text_hi: जैसे ही coil turns करती है, उसके sides square-to-B से tilt away करते हैं, इसलिए हर force shrink होती है और torque sine theta के साथ fall होता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: Coil turns అవుతున్నప్పుడు, దాని sides square-to-B నుండి tilt away అవుతాయి, కాబట్టి ప్రతి force shrink అవుతుంది మరియు torque sine theta తో fall అవుతుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Equal current jumps now give smaller and smaller swings — the marks bunch up into a crowded scale.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Equal current jumps अब छोटे और छोटे swings देते हैं — marks एक crowded scale में bunch up हो जाते हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: Equal current jumps ఇప్పుడు చిన్నవిగా మరియు చిన్నవిగా swings ఇస్తాయి — marks ఒక crowded scale లో bunch up అవుతాయి.

tts_sentences.text_en: In a uniform field the torque is tau equals N I A B sine theta, so the scale is non-uniform and hard to read.
tts_sentences.text_hi: एक uniform field में torque tau equals N I A B sine theta होता है, इसलिए scale non-uniform और hard to read है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఒక uniform field లో torque tau equals N I A B sine theta ఉంటుంది, కాబట్టి scale non-uniform మరియు hard to read.

misconception_watch.belief: Any magnetic field gives a proportional reading — the scale is just whatever it is.
misconception_watch.visual_counter: In a straight uniform field the deflecting-torque arrow shrinks with sinθ as the coil turns, and the crowded-scale ticks bunch together as the current steps up equally.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: In a uniform field τ = NIAB·sinθ, so equal current steps give unequal swings — a crowded, non-linear scale.

STATE_4.title: The radial field: τ = NIAB at every angle

scene_composition.text: Curve the pole faces and slip a soft-iron cylinder inside the coil

What happens to the field lines
in the gap — and to the angle
the coil's sides make with
the field as it turns?

The field bends RADIAL —
always along the core's radius.
The coil plane stays parallel
to B, sides square at every angle.
Radial field: τ = NIAB — no sinθ, no fading    (uniform field was τ = NIAB·sinθ)

tts_sentences.text_en: Now curve the pole faces and slip a soft-iron cylinder inside the coil.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब pole faces को curve करें और coil के अंदर एक soft-iron cylinder डालें।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు pole faces ను curve చేయండి మరియు coil లోపల ఒక soft-iron cylinder ను slip చేయండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: What happens to the field lines in the gap — and to the angle the coil's sides make with the field?
tts_sentences.text_hi: Gap में field lines का क्या होता है — और उस angle का जो coil के sides field के साथ बनाते हैं?
tts_sentences.text_te: Gap లో field lines కు ఏమి జరుగుతుంది — మరియు coil sides field తో చేసే angle కు ఏమి జరుగుతుంది?

tts_sentences.text_en: The field bends radial — every line points along the core's radius. The pole faces curve concave; the core brightens.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Field radial bend होता है — हर line core के radius के along point करती है। Pole faces concave curve होते हैं; core brighten होता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: Field radial గా bend అవుతుంది — ప్రతి line core radius వెంట point చేస్తుంది. Pole faces concave గా curve అవుతాయి; core brighten అవుతుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Now turn the coil through any angle: its sides stay square to B, so both forces hold full length.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब coil को किसी भी angle से turn करें: उसके sides B के square stay करते हैं, इसलिए दोनों forces full length hold करते हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు coil ను ఏ angle లోనైనా turn చేయండి: దాని sides B కి square గా stay అవుతాయి, కాబట్టి రెండు forces full length ను hold చేస్తాయి.

tts_sentences.text_en: The torque stays tau equals N I A B at every deflection — no sine theta, no fading.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Torque हर deflection पर tau equals N I A B रहता है — कोई sine theta नहीं, कोई fading नहीं।
tts_sentences.text_te: Torque ప్రతి deflection వద్ద tau equals N I A B గా stay అవుతుంది — sine theta లేదు, fading లేదు.

misconception_watch.belief: The field between the flat magnet poles is uniform and straight.
misconception_watch.visual_counter: The straight field arrows morph to radial, the pole faces reshape concave, and the soft-iron core brightens — the gap field now points along the core's radius everywhere.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: Concave poles plus a soft-iron core make the gap field radial — always along the core's radius, never straight — so the coil's sides stay perpendicular to B and τ stays NIAB.

scene_composition.text: A fine hairspring is fixed to the coil's shaft

As the coil turns,
what does the hairspring
do to the motion?

The spring winds up as the
coil turns, pushing back —
a restoring torque that grows
opposite the deflecting torque.
The further it winds, the harder it pushes:    restoring torque τ = kφ

tts_sentences.text_en: A fine hairspring is fixed to the coil's shaft.
tts_sentences.text_hi: एक fine hairspring coil के shaft से fixed है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఒక fine hairspring coil shaft కు fixed చేయబడింది.

tts_sentences.text_en: As the coil turns, what does the spring do to the motion?
tts_sentences.text_hi: जैसे ही coil turns करती है, spring motion के साथ क्या करती है?
tts_sentences.text_te: Coil turns అవుతున్నప్పుడు, spring motion కు ఏమి చేస్తుంది?

tts_sentences.text_en: The spring winds up as the coil turns, pushing back with a restoring torque that grows opposite the deflecting torque.
tts_sentences.text_hi: coil के turn होने पर spring wind up होती है, restoring torque के साथ वापस push करती है जो deflecting torque के opposite बढ़ता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: coil turn అయినప్పుడు spring wind up అవుతుంది, restoring torque తో వెనక్కి push చేస్తుంది, అది deflecting torque కి opposite గా పెరుగుతుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: The further it winds, the harder it pushes: the restoring torque is tau equals k phi, proportional to the deflection phi.
tts_sentences.text_hi: जितना यह further wind होता है, उतना ही harder push करता है: restoring torque है tau equals k phi, जो deflection phi के proportional है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇది ఎంత further wind అయితే, అంత harder push చేస్తుంది: restoring torque tau equals k phi, ఇది deflection phi కి proportional.

misconception_watch.belief: Nothing stops the coil — it just keeps turning or spins freely.
misconception_watch.visual_counter: A hairspring appears and winds up as the coil turns, while the restoring-torque arrow grows opposite the deflecting-torque arrow, its length tracking the deflection.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: A hairspring supplies a restoring torque τ = kφ that grows with deflection and opposes the turn — the coil does not spin freely.

scene_composition.text: Two torques now act: deflecting NIAB and restoring kφ

One grows with current,
the other grows with deflection.
Where does the coil come
to rest?

It settles where the two
torque arrows are equal —
the pointer lands on a fixed
reading, well short of 90°.
At rest:    NIAB = kφ    →    φ = (NAB/k)·I

tts_sentences.text_en: Two torques now act together: the deflecting torque N I A B and the restoring torque k phi.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब दो torques एक साथ act करते हैं: deflecting torque N I A B और restoring torque k phi।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు రెండు torques ఒకదానితో ఒకటి act చేస్తాయి: deflecting torque N I A B మరియు restoring torque k phi.

tts_sentences.text_en: One grows with current, the other grows with deflection. Where does the coil come to rest?
tts_sentences.text_hi: एक current के साथ बढ़ता है, दूसरा deflection के साथ बढ़ता है। coil rest पर कहाँ आता है?
tts_sentences.text_te: ఒకటి current తో పెరుగుతుంది, మరొకటి deflection తో పెరుగుతుంది. coil ఎక్కడ rest కి వస్తుంది?

tts_sentences.text_en: The coil settles where the two torques are equal in length — the pointer lands on a fixed reading.
tts_sentences.text_hi: coil वहाँ settle होता है जहाँ दोनों torques length में equal होते हैं — pointer एक fixed reading पर land करता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: రెండు torques length లో equal గా ఉన్న చోట coil settle అవుతుంది — pointer ఒక fixed reading పై land అవుతుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: At rest, N I A B equals k phi, so phi equals N A B over k, times I.
tts_sentences.text_hi: rest पर, N I A B equals k phi, तो phi equals N A B over k, times I।
tts_sentences.text_te: rest వద్ద, N I A B equals k phi, కాబట్టి phi equals N A B over k, times I.

misconception_watch.belief: The coil rotates all the way to 90°, until its moment μ aligns with B.
misconception_watch.visual_counter: The coil damped-settles at a small angle where the deflecting and restoring torque arrows become equal length, and the pointer lands on a tick well short of 90°.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: The coil stops at equilibrium NIAB = kφ — a small reading angle set by the spring, not at full 90° alignment.

scene_composition.text: The real galvanometer scale — the old crowded scale recalled small beside it

Step the current up in equal
jumps again, now in the radial
field — equal steps, or
crowded like before?

Each equal jump in current
moves the pointer the same
amount — the marks are
evenly spaced.
Constant torque → φ = (NAB/k)·I — exactly proportional → a uniform, evenly-spaced scale

tts_sentences.text_en: Here is the real galvanometer scale, with the old crowded scale recalled small beside it.
tts_sentences.text_hi: यह real galvanometer scale है, जिसमें old crowded scale इसके बगल में small recall किया गया है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇది real galvanometer scale, దాని పక్కన old crowded scale small గా recall చేయబడింది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Step the current up in equal jumps again, now in the radial field — equal steps, or crowded like before?
tts_sentences.text_hi: current को फिर से equal jumps में step up करें, अब radial field में — equal steps, या पहले की तरह crowded?
tts_sentences.text_te: current ని మళ్ళీ equal jumps లో step up చేయండి, ఇప్పుడు radial field లో — equal steps, లేదా ముందులా crowded గా?

tts_sentences.text_en: Each equal jump in current moves the pointer the same amount — the marks are evenly spaced.
tts_sentences.text_hi: current में हर equal jump pointer को same amount से move करता है — marks evenly spaced होते हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: current లో ప్రతి equal jump pointer ని same amount తో move చేస్తుంది — marks evenly spaced ఉంటాయి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Because the torque was constant, phi equals N A B over k times I — exactly proportional, so the scale is uniform.
tts_sentences.text_hi: क्योंकि torque constant था, phi equals N A B over k times I — exactly proportional, इसलिए scale uniform है।
tts_sentences.text_te: torque constant కాబట్టి, phi equals N A B over k times I — exactly proportional, కాబట్టి scale uniform.

misconception_watch.belief: The scale is crowded and non-uniform, just like the uniform-field case.
misconception_watch.visual_counter: On the radial-field scale the pointer steps in equal angular increments as the current steps equally, shown beside the recalled crowded scale from STATE_3.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: The radial field gives constant torque, so φ ∝ I exactly — the marks are evenly spaced, a uniform scale, not crowded.

scene_composition.text: Sensitivity: the swing you get per unit current, φ ÷ I

To make the meter detect an
even tinier current, what
would you change about
the device?

Add turns N, widen area A,
strengthen field B, or soften
spring k — the same current
swings the pointer further.
Current sensitivity φ/I = NAB/k — a fixed device constant, never the current measured

tts_sentences.text_en: Sensitivity is the swing you get per unit current — the deflection phi divided by the current I.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Sensitivity वह swing है जो आपको प्रति unit current मिलता है — deflection phi divided by current I.
tts_sentences.text_te: Sensitivity అంటే మీకు ప్రతి unit current కు వచ్చే swing — deflection phi divided by current I.

tts_sentences.text_en: To make the meter detect an even tinier current, what would you change about it?
tts_sentences.text_hi: Meter को और भी छोटे current को detect करने के लिए, आप इसमें क्या बदलेंगे?
tts_sentences.text_te: మీటర్ ను మరింత చిన్న current ను detect చేయడానికి, మీరు దానిలో ఏమి మారుస్తారు?

tts_sentences.text_en: Add more turns N, widen the area A, strengthen the field B, or soften the spring k — the same current swings further.
tts_sentences.text_hi: ज़्यादा turns N जोड़ें, area A को चौड़ा करें, field B को मज़बूत करें, या spring k को नरम करें — वही current ज़्यादा swing करता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఎక్కువ turns N జోడించండి, area A ని వెడల్పు చేయండి, field B ని బలంగా చేయండి, లేదా spring k ని మృదువుగా చేయండి — అదే current మరింత swing చేస్తుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Current sensitivity is phi over I equals N A B over k — a fixed device constant, never depending on the current measured.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Current sensitivity है phi over I equals N A B over k — एक fixed device constant, कभी भी measured current पर depend नहीं करता।
tts_sentences.text_te: Current sensitivity అంటే phi over I equals N A B over k — ఒక fixed device constant, ఎప్పుడూ measured current పై depend చేయదు.

misconception_watch.belief: Sensitivity depends on how big a current you measure.
misconception_watch.visual_counter: Varying N, A, B or k changes how far the pointer swings for the same current; varying the current alone leaves the ratio φ/I unchanged.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: Current sensitivity φ/I = NAB/k is a fixed device constant — it depends on N, A, B and k, never on the current being measured.

STATE_9.title: Your turn — I, N, B, A, k

scene_composition.text: Explore: drag current I, turns N, field B, area A, spring k

Watch the deflection φ and
the current sensitivity update
live on the uniform scale
as you drag.

Challenge:
Double the turns N — does φ double?
Soften the spring k — what
happens to the swing?
More N, A or B, or a softer k, raise the sensitivity; more current swings the pointer further.

tts_sentences.text_en: Your turn. Drag current I, turns N, field B, area A, and spring constant k.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब आपकी बारी। Drag करें current I, turns N, field B, area A, और spring constant k।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు మీ వంతు. Drag చేయండి current I, turns N, field B, area A, మరియు spring constant k.

tts_sentences.text_en: Watch the deflection phi and the current sensitivity update live on the uniform scale.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Deflection phi और current sensitivity को uniform scale पर live update होते देखिए।
tts_sentences.text_te: Deflection phi మరియు current sensitivity ను uniform scale పై live update అవుతూ చూడండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: More turns, bigger area, stronger field, or a softer spring all raise the sensitivity; more current swings the pointer further.
tts_sentences.text_hi: ज़्यादा turns, बड़ा area, मज़बूत field, या नरम spring — सब sensitivity बढ़ाते हैं; ज़्यादा current pointer को और आगे swing करता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఎక్కువ turns, పెద్ద area, బలమైన field, లేదా మృదువైన spring — అన్నీ sensitivity ని పెంచుతాయి; ఎక్కువ current pointer ను మరింత ముందుకు swing చేస్తుంది.

STATE_1.label: A coil between magnet poles — current marching, pointer at zero
STATE_1.caption: An N-turn coil on a soft-iron core, between two magnet poles. Current I marches; the pointer rests at zero.

STATE_2.label: The couple: F = NBIL each side, ΣF = 0, τ = NIAB
STATE_2.caption: Each side: F = NBIL. Equal-opposite on different lines → ΣF = 0 (no slide), deflecting torque τ = NIAB.

STATE_3.label: Uniform field: τ = NIAB·sinθ → a crowded scale
STATE_3.caption: In a straight uniform field τ = NIAB·sinθ: equal current steps give shrinking swings, so the scale crowds.

STATE_4.label: Radial field morph: τ = NIAB at every angle (PRIMARY AHA)
STATE_4.caption: Concave poles + soft-iron core bend the field radial: the coil's sides stay square to B, so τ stays NIAB — no sinθ fade.

STATE_5.label: The hairspring winds: restoring torque τ = kφ
STATE_5.caption: A hairspring winds as the coil turns, pushing back with a restoring torque τ = kφ, opposite the deflecting torque.

STATE_6.label: Equilibrium: the coil settles where NIAB = kφ
STATE_6.caption: The coil damped-settles where the deflecting and restoring torques balance: NIAB = kφ → φ = (NAB/k)·I.

STATE_7.label: Uniform scale: equal current steps, equal swings (SUPPORTING AHA)
STATE_7.caption: Radial field → constant torque → φ = (NAB/k)·I: equal current steps move the pointer in equal steps — a uniform scale.

STATE_8.label: Current sensitivity φ/I = NAB/k — raise N, A, B or lower k
STATE_8.caption: Sensitivity φ/I = NAB/k: raise turns N, area A, field B, or soften spring k — the same current swings further.

STATE_9.label: Sandbox — I, N, B, A, k sliders with a live φ + sensitivity readout
STATE_9.caption: Drag I, N, B, A, k. Deflection φ = (NAB/k)·I and current sensitivity φ/I = NAB/k update live on the uniform scale.

regeneration_variants.label: Analog needle multimeter
regeneration_variants.physical_world: The swinging needle on an electrician's non-digital multimeter or a battery tester
regeneration_variants.same_physics: The same moving coil on a radial-field magnet turns against a hairspring; NIAB = kφ gives φ = (NAB/k)·I, so the needle reads current on an evenly-spaced scale
regeneration_variants.scene_hint: A rectangular panel meter, the same coil-core-magnet trio behind the dial, the needle stepping equally as the current steps.

regeneration_variants.label: VU meter on an audio amplifier
regeneration_variants.physical_world: The bouncing needle VU meter on an old audio amplifier or tape deck
regeneration_variants.same_physics: A moving coil in a radial field, balanced by a hairspring, deflects in proportion to the signal current — the same φ = (NAB/k)·I on a uniform scale
regeneration_variants.scene_hint: A VU meter face, the needle riding the music; the same hidden coil-core-magnet and the same uniform tick spacing.

assessment.mastery_definition: A student has mastered this concept when they can explain that a moving-coil galvanometer reads current because the BIL force on the coil's two vertical sides forms a COUPLE: equal-opposite forces on different lines give ΣF = 0 (the coil turns in place, it never slides) and a deflecting torque τ = NIAB; that in a plain UNIFORM field τ = NIAB·sinθ, so equal current steps give unequal swings and the scale would be crowded; that the RADIAL field made by concave pole faces and a soft-iron core keeps the coil plane always parallel to B, so its sides stay perpendicular to B at every angle and τ stays NIAB with no sinθ fade — the key trick of the instrument; that a hairspring supplies a restoring torque τ = kφ, so the coil rests at equilibrium where NIAB = kφ — a small reading angle, never at 90°; that this gives φ = (NAB/k)·I, exactly proportional to current, which is precisely why the galvanometer scale is uniform (evenly spaced); and that the current sensitivity φ/I = NAB/k is a fixed device constant, raised by more turns N, a bigger area A, a stronger field B, or a softer spring k, and never depending on the current being measured. (Converting the galvanometer into an ammeter or voltmeter, and voltage sensitivity φ/V = NAB/kR, are downstream concepts not assessed by this conceptual V1 quiz.)

questions.stem: Current flows through the coil of a galvanometer sitting between the magnet poles. What does the magnetic force do to the coil?

options.A: ΣF = 0, but τ = NIAB — the two side-forces form a couple, so the coil turns in place without sliding
options.B: The coil slides sideways across the gap, pushed by a net magnetic force
options.C: The net force is zero, so the coil feels nothing and stays exactly at rest
options.D: Force and torque are the same thing here, so the coil both slides and turns

distractor_misconceptions.B: Thinks the side-forces add to a net force that translates the coil across the gap (ignores that they are on different lines)
distractor_misconceptions.C: Conflates ΣF = 0 with Στ = 0 — assumes zero net force means nothing happens, missing the couple
distractor_misconceptions.D: Does not distinguish force from torque, so expects both sliding and turning

questions.tested_idea: The two side-forces form a couple: ΣF = 0 (no slide) but τ = NIAB (pure turning).
questions.parallel_form_stem: A rectangular current loop hangs between two magnet poles and current is switched on. Describe the net force and the torque on the loop, and say whether it slides or turns.

questions.stem: Imagine a galvanometer built with a plain UNIFORM field between flat poles. You raise the current in equal steps. What does the scale look like?

options.A: Non-uniform: because τ = NIAB·sinθ, equal current steps give smaller and smaller swings, so the marks crowd together
options.B: Uniform: the torque stays constant in a uniform field, so equal steps give equal swings
options.C: Blank at first: the current is too small to read until it gets large
options.D: Evenly spaced but faint: the field is simply too weak to move the pointer cleanly

distractor_misconceptions.B: Thinks the torque is constant in a uniform field, ignoring the sinθ factor that fades as the coil turns
distractor_misconceptions.C: Blames the current magnitude rather than the angle-dependent torque
distractor_misconceptions.D: Blames the field strength rather than the sinθ dependence of the torque

questions.tested_idea: In a uniform field τ = NIAB·sinθ, so equal current steps give unequal swings — a crowded scale.
questions.parallel_form_stem: A coil deflects in a straight, uniform magnetic field. As it turns further, what happens to the torque on it, and what does that do to a scale marked in equal current steps?

questions.stem: Curving the pole faces and adding a soft-iron core makes the gap field radial. What does this achieve for the torque on the coil?

options.A: It keeps the coil's sides perpendicular to B at every angle, so τ = NIAB stays constant as the coil turns
options.B: It makes the torque larger than it would be in a uniform field
options.C: It cancels the forces on the coil's two sides
options.D: It rotates the coil's magnetic moment μ until μ lines up with B

distractor_misconceptions.B: Thinks radial means stronger; a constant torque is not a larger one
distractor_misconceptions.C: Thinks the radial field removes the side-forces, when the forces persist and stay full length
distractor_misconceptions.D: Thinks μ aligns with B, when the radial field keeps μ perpendicular to B at every deflection

questions.tested_idea: The radial field keeps the coil's sides ⊥ B at every angle, so τ = NIAB is independent of deflection.
questions.parallel_form_stem: A galvanometer uses concave poles and an iron core so the field is radial. Explain how that keeps the deflecting torque the same at every deflection angle.

questions.stem: With both a deflecting torque NIAB and a hairspring's restoring torque kφ acting, at what deflection does the coil come to rest?

options.A: Where the deflecting torque NIAB equals the restoring torque kφ
options.B: At 90°, where the coil's magnetic moment μ lines up with the field B
options.C: It never comes to rest — it keeps turning freely
options.D: At 0°, because the spring always pulls it straight back to zero

distractor_misconceptions.B: Thinks the coil swings to full 90° alignment, ignoring that the spring stops it at a small angle
distractor_misconceptions.C: Thinks nothing opposes the turn, missing the restoring torque kφ
distractor_misconceptions.D: Thinks the spring wins completely, ignoring the deflecting torque NIAB

questions.tested_idea: The coil rests at equilibrium where NIAB = kφ — a small reading angle, not 90°.
questions.parallel_form_stem: A current-carrying coil twists against a hairspring. Write the condition that decides the angle at which it finally stops.

questions.stem: In the real radial-field galvanometer, how does the deflection φ depend on the current I, and why is the scale spaced the way it is?

options.A: φ = (NAB/k)·I — deflection is exactly proportional to current, so the marks are evenly spaced (a uniform scale)
options.B: The scale is crowded, just like the plain uniform-field case
options.C: φ is proportional to I², so the marks spread further apart at higher currents
options.D: φ is proportional to √I, so the marks crowd together at higher currents

distractor_misconceptions.B: Carries the crowded uniform-field scale over to the radial case, missing that the torque is now constant
distractor_misconceptions.C: Invents a square-law dependence instead of the linear φ = (NAB/k)·I
distractor_misconceptions.D: Invents a square-root dependence instead of the linear φ = (NAB/k)·I

questions.tested_idea: Constant torque gives φ = (NAB/k)·I, linear in current — an evenly-spaced uniform scale.
questions.parallel_form_stem: A galvanometer's pointer steps in equal angles as the current steps in equal amounts. Write the relation between deflection and current that makes this happen.

questions.stem: You want the galvanometer to detect an even smaller current. The current sensitivity is φ/I. What is it, and how do you increase it?

options.A: φ/I = NAB/k — raise the turns N, the area A, or the field B, or soften the spring k
options.B: It depends on how big a current you happen to be measuring
options.C: φ/I = NAB/k — so raise the spring constant k to make it more sensitive
options.D: It depends on how long the pointer needle is

distractor_misconceptions.B: Thinks sensitivity changes with the current measured, when φ/I is a fixed device constant
distractor_misconceptions.C: Gets the sign wrong: a stiffer (larger k) spring lowers sensitivity; you must soften k
distractor_misconceptions.D: Blames pointer geometry, which does not enter φ/I = NAB/k

questions.tested_idea: Current sensitivity φ/I = NAB/k is a device constant, raised by larger N, A, B or smaller k.
questions.parallel_form_stem: Two galvanometers carry the same tiny current but one deflects further. Write what sets the deflection-per-unit-current and name two changes that increase it.
